headline: A dense MLP retrofitted with actual router logits plus Triton block-sparse execution beats dense router+MLP on GB10 medium transformer shapes and in a Llama-like decoder prefill harness, while preserving a masked dense-router reference.
limitations: The decoder layer is a local Llama-like harness, not a full checkpoint retrofit.
Correctness is against a masked dense-router reference; original unmasked model quality is not established.
Router thresholds are calibrated to active-block fractions on synthetic random activations, not trained on language validation loss.
Very small shapes failed speed gates: the smoke test passed correctness but routed module speedup was below 1.0 because overhead dominated.
Shorter repeat counts than a publication-grade benchmark; enough for local go/no-go but not final scientific closure.
next_steps: Integrate the routed module into one real transformer FFN checkpoint or an open MoE-to-dense distillation target and evaluate validation loss/perplexity.
Train or distill router thresholds/logits on language activations instead of random hidden states.
Profile kernel launch overhead and fuse router/build/MLP boundaries for small-token regimes, or gate deployment to medium/large prefill shapes.
Add energy/J-per-token telemetry if choosing between dense and routed paths at serving time.
